summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI technology, regulation, and adoption. Key stories include the EU's order for Google to open Android and Search to rivals, NVIDIA's introduction of new Jetson Thor computers, and the growing concern over AI-generated child sexual abuse material. Meanwhile, companies like OpenAI and Anthropic continue to innovate and expand their offerings.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries and applications has led to a growing need for regulation and oversight, as evidenced by the EU's recent orders and the rising concerns over AI-generated CSAM.
highlights: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
NVIDIA introduces new Jetson Thor computers for mainstream robotics and edge AI
OpenAI and Anthropic continue to innovate and expand their AI offerings

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenaAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.
